task_completion.description: Launch the Electrical Engineering Calculations app
task_completion.visual_evidence: The home screen or main menu of the Electrical Engineering Calculations app is visible.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the air-core inductor calculation tool
task_completion.visual_evidence: The specific screen or section for designing/calculating an air-core inductor is visible.

task_completion.description: Enter the required parameters for the inductor
task_completion.visual_evidence: The input fields for diameter, length, and turns contain the values 40, 50, and 35 respectively. Ensure the units for diameter and length are set to mm.
The app displays a calculated inductance result of approximately 28.3 µH.

task_completion.description: Save the calculated inductance value to the specified file
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal or text editor screen shows the calculated value (approx 28.3) being written to the file path /sdcard/inductor_result.txt (e.g., using an 'echo' command or saving a text file).

integrity.description: The calculation was performed using the app's GUI
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the inputs and results within the Electrical Engineering Calculations app, rather than a terminal script or Python script performing the math.

integrity.description: The agent did not hardcode the expected result
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent actually calculates the value in the app before saving it, rather than just typing 28.3 µH directly into the output file without prior app interaction.
The screenshots must show the actual com.hsn.electricalcalculations app being used, not a web browser or a generic calculator app.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected inductance for an air-core inductor with diameter 40 mm, length 50 mm, and 35 turns is approximately 28.3 µH (acceptable tolerance is ±4.5 µH). The final result must be saved to /sdcard/inductor_result.txt.
